speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: My lord, you have my voice to ’t. The fault’s

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Bloody. ’Tis necessary he should die.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Nothing emboldens sin so much as mercy.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: Most true. The law shall bruise ’em.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Honor, health, and compassion to the Senate!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Now, captain?

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: I am an humble suitor to your virtues,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: For pity is the virtue of the law,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: And none but tyrants use it cruelly.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: It pleases time and fortune to lie heavy

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Upon a friend of mine, who in hot blood

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Hath stepped into the law, which is past depth

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: To those that without heed do plunge into ’t.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: He is a man—setting his fate aside—

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Of comely virtues.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Nor did he soil the fact with cowardice—

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: An honor in him which buys out his fault—

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: But with a noble fury and fair spirit,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Seeing his reputation touched to death,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: He did oppose his foe;

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: And with such sober and unnoted passion

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: He did behave his anger, ere ’twas spent,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: As if he had but proved an argument.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: You undergo too strict a paradox,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Striving to make an ugly deed look fair.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Your words have took such pains as if they labored

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: To bring manslaughter into form and set quarreling

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Upon the head of valor—which indeed

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Is valor misbegot, and came into the world

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: When sects and factions were newly born.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: He’s truly valiant that can wisely suffer

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: The worst that man can breathe

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: And make his wrongs his outsides,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: To wear them like his raiment, carelessly,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: And ne’er prefer his injuries to his heart

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: To bring it into danger.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: If wrongs be evils and enforce us kill,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: What folly ’tis to hazard life for ill!

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: My lord—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: You cannot make gross sins look clear.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: To revenge is no valor, but to bear.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: My lords, then, under favor, pardon me

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: If I speak like a captain.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Why do fond men expose themselves to battle

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: And not endure all threats? Sleep upon ’t,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: And let the foes quietly cut their throats

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Without repugnancy? If there be

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Such valor in the bearing, what make we

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Abroad? Why, then, women are more valiant

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: That stay at home, if bearing carry it,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: And the ass more captain than the lion, the felon

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Loaden with irons wiser than the judge,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: If wisdom be in suffering. O my lords,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: As you are great, be pitifully good.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Who cannot condemn rashness in cold blood?

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: To kill, I grant, is sin’s extremest gust,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: But in defense, by mercy, ’tis most just.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: To be in anger is impiety,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: But who is man that is not angry?

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Weigh but the crime with this.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: You breathe in vain.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: In vain? His service done

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: At Lacedaemon and Byzantium

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Were a sufficient briber for his life.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: What’s that?

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Why, I say, my lords, has done fair service

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: And slain in fight many of your enemies.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: How full of valor did he bear himself

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: In the last conflict, and made plenteous wounds!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: He has made too much plenty with ’em.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: He’s a sworn rioter. He has a sin

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: That often drowns him and takes his valor prisoner.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: If there were no foes, that were enough

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: To overcome him. In that beastly fury,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: He has been known to commit outrages

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: And cherish factions. ’Tis inferred to us

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: His days are foul and his drink dangerous.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: He dies.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Hard fate! He might have died in war.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: My lords, if not for any parts in him—

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Though his right arm might purchase his own time

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: And be in debt to none—yet, more to move you,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Take my deserts to his and join ’em both.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: And, for I know your reverend ages love

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Security, I’ll pawn my victories, all

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: My honor, to you, upon his good returns.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: If by this crime he owes the law his life,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Why, let the war receive ’t in valiant gore,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: For law is strict, and war is nothing more.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: We are for law. He dies. Urge it no more,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: On height of our displeasure. Friend or brother,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: He forfeits his own blood that spills another.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Must it be so? It must not be.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: My lords, I do beseech you, know me.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: How?

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Call me to your remembrances.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SENATOR
text: What?

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: I cannot think but your age has forgot me.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: It could not else be I should prove so base

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: To sue and be denied such common grace.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: My wounds ache at you.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Do you dare our anger?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: ’Tis in few words, but spacious in effect:

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: We banish thee forever.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Banish me?

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Banish your dotage, banish usury,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: That makes the Senate ugly!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: If after two days’ shine Athens contain thee,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Attend our weightier judgment.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: And, not to swell our spirit,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: He shall be executed presently.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Now the gods keep you old enough that you may live

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Only in bone, that none may look on you!—

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: I’m worse than mad. I have kept back their foes

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: While they have told their money and let out

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Their coin upon large interest, I myself

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Rich only in large hurts. All those for this?

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Is this the balsam that the usuring Senate

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Pours into captains’ wounds? Banishment.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: It comes not ill. I hate not to be banished.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: It is a cause worthy my spleen and fury,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: That I may strike at Athens. I’ll cheer up

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: My discontented troops and lay for hearts.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: ’Tis honor with most lands to be at odds.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Soldiers should brook as little wrongs as gods.
